meaning: n.<新西兰>(石灰岩地体上的)灰岩坑,灰岩洞
examples: Tomo: How was the party last night?
If he was Jerry Tomo, he probably would have excused himself.
The Conference hotel Tomo is a new hotel offering a wide range of high-quality services in a quiet suburb of Riga, a 10-minute drive from the city centre.
Locals have to go into the bush to farm small plots of land, a dangerous necessity, explains camp leader Tomo Kello(ph).
One of Yuzawa's meru tomo could well be Koji Hakuta.
